avalancheActivity.highlights: Gradual increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation small and medium-sized moist and wet avalanches are possible in all altitude zones. More recent wind slabs are clearly recognisable. The wind slabs can be released, especially by large additional loads, in particular on steep shady slopes. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls.
Weak layers exist in the old snowpack on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. Avalanches can be triggered in the old snowpack reach medium size in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
These spring-like weather conditions from the late morning will give rise to moistening of the snowpack in all aspects. The various wind slabs have bonded with each other and the old snowpack.
Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes. Avalanches can in some places be released in deep layers in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.comment:
The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften during the day. As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation moist and wet avalanches are possible as the day progresses, but they will be mostly small. The more recent wind slabs are easy to recognise and can be released by large loads at their margins in particular.
Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground.

snowpackStructure.comment: The high temperatures from the late morning will give rise to increasing softening of the snowpack in some cases in particular on sunny slopes.
